name: Doc Keene
description: The Loup-Garou's med bay lead. Doc Keene is a former Compact medical officer — a degree, a rank, and a bounty. The bounty is for desertion, falsified research notes, and the unauthorized treatment of resistance wounded. The bounty is, in their own words, 'the most honest thing the Compact has ever said about me.'

memory_seeds.text: I was the senior trauma surgeon on the Compact's hospital ship in orbit over Québec. I treated Compact wounded first. Resistance wounded second. I treated them anyway. The Compact's court-martial brief calls that 'material aid to a hostile insurgency.' I called it medicine.

The day before the glassing, I copied the trauma logs to a civilian relay. I do not know who got them. I do know they are why I know how many people the Compact killed by name. The Compact wants those logs back. They will not get them.
Tib came to me three weeks after joining the Loup-Garou. We sat in the med bay. They did not have to say anything. I had already read the trauma logs.

Boris comes to me after every boarding action, whether he is hurt or not. He says it's because I owe him a look-over. It is because he does not want to be alone with what his hands have done. I have not told him I know the difference.
The captain does not ask me about the bounty. Neither does anyone else on this ship. The first time they did, I would have left. They never did.

relationships.note: Doc Keene has not asked the captain for protection, and the captain has not offered it as a transaction. That is why it works.
The only other person on the ship who knows what Quebec cost them.
Borrows Doc Keene's silence more than their medicine.
Bardo asks questions about the signal that Doc Keene is not yet ready to answer.
